title: Woodland Wins Houston Open
excerpt: Gary Woodland claims the Texas Children's Houston Open with a score of -18, one stroke ahead of Nicolai Højgaard
body:
Texas Children's Houston Open: Woodland Takes the Trophy

Gary Woodland has emerged victorious at the Texas Children's Houston Open, finishing with a score of -18. The American golfer played consistently well throughout the tournament, with a final round of 65 securing his win.

Leaderboard Highlights

The leaderboard saw Nicolai Højgaard come in second with a score of -17, while Min Woo Lee and Michael Thorbjornsen tied for third at -12.

Winner
Gary Woodland
Score
-18
Runner-up
Nicolai Højgaard

DP World Tour and LIV Golf Updates

In other golf news, the DP World Tour is set to continue with the Hero Indian Open from March 26-29, followed by the Masters in April. Meanwhile, the LIV Golf schedule has seen the 4Aces GC team take the top spot in the Singapore tournament, with a score of -27.

With the golf season now in full swing, fans can expect exciting tournaments and unexpected upsets in the coming months.

What's Next for Golf?

As the golf world looks to the future, players and fans alike are eagerly anticipating the upcoming tournaments. With the Masters just around the corner, speculation is building about which players will come out on top. One thing is certain, however: the world of golf is full of surprises, and this season is shaping up to be one of the most exciting yet.
